parsed.summary: The commit renames the npm scope and adds an interactive setup menu. The scope rename from @anthropic to @anthropic-ai (or similar) in package.json is a breaking change for existing users who depend on the old package name. The setup.md changes introduce interactive prompts without clear handling for non-interactive or CI environments, risking silent hangs or failures. The release_card.md changes are extensive but appear to be documentation-only.

findings.title: npm scope rename is an undeclared breaking change
findings.body: The package.json change renames the npm scope. Any existing user with `@anthropic/cli` (or old scope) in their package.json, lockfile, or CI pipeline will break on upgrade. There is no migration guide, no deprecation path, and no mention of backwards compatibility in the CHANGELOG. Published packages under the old scope will become orphaned; new installs under the new scope will be a fresh package with no version history. This can cause silent dependency resolution failures in monorepos or pinned deployments.
findings.recommendation: Publish the new-scoped package as a new major version with a clear migration guide. Keep the old-scoped package published with a postinstall warning pointing to the new scope. Add a note in CHANGELOG.md explicitly marking this as a breaking change.

findings.title: Interactive setup menu has no non-interactive/CI guard
findings.body: The commands/setup.md changes add an interactive menu when already configured. If this command is invoked in a CI/CD pipeline, headless environment, or non-TTY context, the interactive prompt will either hang indefinitely or error with no useful message. There is no `--yes`/`--no-input` flag visible, and no detection of TTY absence.
findings.recommendation: Add a non-interactive mode flag (e.g., `--force` or `--non-interactive`) that skips the menu and applies defaults. Detect non-TTY contexts and either exit with a clear error or auto-select a sensible default.

findings.title: CHANGELOG does not call out the scope rename as breaking
findings.body: The CHANGELOG entry for v0.4.7 describes the interactive menu addition but does not mention the npm scope rename or its impact on existing installations. Users reading the changelog will not be warned that upgrading requires changing their dependency references.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'BREAKING CHANGE' section to the v0.4.7 changelog entry explicitly documenting the scope rename and required user action.

next_steps: Inspect the full diff of package.json to confirm the exact old and new scope names and assess blast radius.
Verify whether the old-scoped package has existing dependents on npm.
Add TTY detection or a --non-interactive flag to the setup command before shipping.
Update CHANGELOG.md with a breaking change notice for the scope rename.
